1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-$0$ curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean
4	
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	
10	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
11	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
12	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
13	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
14	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
15	% keystone directly). The headline thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
16	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
17	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
18	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
19	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
20	
21	The genus-$0$ arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
22	($J = \Spec k$); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
23	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$
24	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
25	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field $k$, so the
26	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
27	O(-2)) = 0$, hence $df = 0$, hence $f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
28	characteristic-$p$ Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant $f$ can have $df = 0$. The
29	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
30	
31	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
32	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
33	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
34	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
35	derives the genus-$0$ base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
36	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
37	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
38	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that $\mathbb P^1$ is
39	\emph{already complete} and $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
40	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
41	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut} (the project's
42	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
43	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
44	\mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (the M\"obius scaling fixing both $0$ and
45	$\infty$), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
46	G_m$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising $f(0) = \eta_A$ and composing $h := \sigma_\times
47	\fatsemi f$, one applies additivity of $\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
48	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
49	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ proper, $W = \mathbb G_m$, and
50	base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$. Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of
51	scaling, the $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)$ is
52	\emph{constant} at $\eta_A$, so the decomposition collapses to $h = p \fatsemi f$, i.e.\
53	$f(\lambda x) = f(x)$. Specialising $x = 1$ gives that $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant, and since
54	$\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ with $A$ separated, $f$ is constant
55	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
56	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
57	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
58	$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$, and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
59	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical $\mathbb G_a$-additive
60	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility
61	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
62	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$ critical path. The corollary that a
63	pointed regular map of abelian
64	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
65	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-$0$ path; Milne's
66	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
67	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-$0$ critical path
68	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
69	gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant
70	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-$0$
71	curves (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}, $C \cong \mathbb P^1$ over $\bar k$) yields the
72	headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
73	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
74	neither Serre duality nor the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is
75	fully decoupled from Route~A.
76	
77	\medskip
78	
79	Throughout, $\bar k$ is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
80	\emph{abelian variety} over $\bar k$ is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
81	project's Lean encoding $A$ is an object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ carrying
82	$\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, and $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$.
83	
84	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
85	
86	\begin{lemma}
87	
88	\leanok
89	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
90	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
92	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
93	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let $X$ be a complete variety, $Y$ and $Z$
94	  % any varieties, and $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ a morphism such that for some
95	  % $y_0 \in Y$, $f(X \times \{y_0\})$ is a single point $z_0$ of $Z$. Then there is a
96	  % morphism $g \colon Y \to Z$ such that if $p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y$ is the
97	  % projection, $f = g \circ p_2$."
98	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
99	  Let $X$ be a complete variety, and let $Y$, $Z$ be any varieties over $\bar k$. Let
100	  $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ be a morphism such that for some $y_0 \in Y$ the image

input.description: Find blueprint sections for genus0 base objects

content:
900	  itself, so the additive decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} applies directly.
901	  Consequently \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends} (Theorem~3.2), the codim-$1$ indeterminacy
902	  sub-build (Lemma~3.3), and the Weil-divisor gap discussed above are all confined to
903	  \textbf{Route~A} (the positive-genus Albanese universal property, Milne \S III.6); they are
904	  retained in this chapter solely as Route-A inputs and may be deferred until Route~A consumes
905	  them. The genus-$0$ close no longer depends on any of them.
906	\end{remark}
907	
908	\section{The genus-$0$ base objects and the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action (the primary route)}
909	
910	\begin{definition}
911	[Concrete genus-$0$ base objects: $\mathbb P^1$, $\mathbb G_a$, $\mathbb G_m$ over $\bar k$]
912	  \label{def:genus0_base_objects}
913	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}
914	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of standard objects; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
915	  % I.2--3 for the projective line; the group laws on $\mathbb A^1$ are elementary). No external
916	  % % SOURCE QUOTE is needed for the Lean encoding itself.
917	  Work over $\Spec \bar k$ with $\bar k$ algebraically closed. The primary $\mathbb G_m$-scaling
918	  route (and its $\mathbb G_a$-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of
919	  $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$:
920	  \begin{itemize}
921	    \item $\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, the projective line --- a smooth proper
922	      geometrically irreducible curve of genus $0$ (Mathlib: projective space, or $\mathrm{Proj}$
923	      of the graded polynomial ring; \lean name \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar} [expected]). It carries
924	      the distinguished $\bar k$-points $0$, $1$, $\infty$ and the two standard affine charts
925	      $\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$ (coordinate $x$) and
926	      $\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0\}$ (coordinate $u = 1/x$).
927	    \item $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}$, the affine line as the \emph{additive} group object
928	      (Mathlib: \texttt{AffineSpace}\,$\mathbb A(1; \Spec \bar k)$ with the $\mathtt{GrpObj}$
929	      additive structure $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$, identity $0$; \lean name \texttt{Ga} [expected]).
930	      As a scheme $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, and the open
931	      immersion $\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1$ has dense (cofinite) image.
932	    \item $\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0, \infty\}$, the
933	      \emph{multiplicative} group object (group law $(x, y) \mapsto xy$, identity $1$; \lean name
934	      \texttt{Gm} [expected]). Its open immersion into $\mathbb P^1$ also has dense image.
935	  \end{itemize}
936	  All three are of finite type over $\bar k$; $\mathbb P^1$ is proper, while $\mathbb G_a$ and
937	  $\mathbb G_m$ are affine (hence not complete). These are the only ``genus-$0$'' geometric inputs
938	  the base case needs.
939	\end{definition}
940	
941	\begin{definition}
942	[The $\mathbb G_m$-scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1$ (primary), and the $\mathbb G_a$-translation companion $\sigma$]
943	  \label{def:gaTranslationP1}
944	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1}
945	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
946	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the chart computations below are elementary and characteristic-free.
947	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE for the encoding. The mathematical fact -- $\mathbb G_m$ acts on
948	  % $\mathbb P^1$ by scalings fixing $0$ and $\infty$, $\mathbb G_a$ by translations fixing $\infty$
949	  % -- is standard (the Borel subgroup of $\mathrm{PGL}_2$ acting on $\mathbb P^1$).
950	  % NOTE (iter-164): the load-bearing action for the PRIMARY genus-0 route is now the MULTIPLICATIVE
951	  % scaling action $\sigma_\times$ (\lean name gmScalingP1 [expected]); the $\mathbb G_a$-translation
952	  % $\sigma$ (\lean name gaTranslationP1 [expected]) is retained only for the demoted additive
953	  % alternative. Both expected Lean targets are listed in the writer report for scaffolding.
954	  \textbf{The scaling action (primary).} The \emph{multiplicative scaling action}
955	  \[ \sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
956	     \text{``}\sigma_\times(x, \lambda) = \lambda x\text{''} \]
957	  (\lean name \texttt{gmScalingP1} [expected]) is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of
958	  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ --- no indeterminacy, characteristic-free (M\"obius scaling
959	  fixing both $0$ and $\infty$) --- defined chartwise:
960	  \begin{itemize}
961	    \item On $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_m$ (target chart $x$): $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$,
962	      a polynomial map, regular everywhere.
963	    \item Near $\infty$ (target chart $u = 1/x$): the target coordinate $1/(\lambda x)$ rewrites as
964	      $u/\lambda$, regular everywhere on this chart because $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$ is invertible.
965	  \end{itemize}
966	  The two target charts cover the image and overlap consistently, so $\sigma_\times$ is defined on
967	  the whole surface. It is a left action of the group $\mathbb G_m$ on $\mathbb P^1$:
968	  $\sigma_\times(x, 1) = x$ and $\sigma_\times(\sigma_\times(x, \lambda), \mu) = \sigma_\times(x,
969	  \lambda\mu)$. Crucially it has \emph{two fixed points}, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ and
970	  $\sigma_\times(\infty, \lambda) = \infty$ for all $\lambda$; the fixed point $0$ is what makes the
971	  $W$-axis of the additive decomposition collapse in the primary base-case proof
972	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}).
973	
974	  \medskip
975	  \noindent\textbf{Companion: the $\mathbb G_a$-translation action (demoted route only).} For the
976	  Milne-faithful additive alternative we also record the \emph{translation action}
977	  \[ \sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to \mathbb P^1, \qquad
978	     \text{``}\sigma(x, y) = x + y\text{''} \]
979	  (\lean name \texttt{gaTranslationP1} [expected]), likewise a \emph{total} scheme morphism on all
980	  of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a$, defined chartwise: on $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb G_a$
981	  (chart $x$) it is the polynomial map $(x, y) \mapsto x + y$; near $\infty$ ($u = 1/x$) the
982	  coordinate $1/(x + y)$ rewrites as $u/(1 + u y)$, regular at $u = 0$ (value $0$ there --- $\sigma$
983	  fixes $\infty$ for every $y$), while the locus $x + y = 0$ is covered by the chart $x$ on which it
984	  is the finite value $0$. It is a left action of $\mathbb G_a$ on $\mathbb P^1$ fixing $\infty$:
985	  $\sigma(x, 0) = x$ and $\sigma(\sigma(x, y), y') = \sigma(x, y + y')$. This companion underlies
986	  only the alternative proof of \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} and the demoted $\mathbb
987	  G_a$-additive close of the base case (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}); it is \emph{not} on the
988	  genus-$0$ critical path.
989	\end{definition}
990	
991	\begin{lemma}
992	[$\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$: a homomorphism from the additive group into an abelian variety is trivial]
993	  \label{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
994	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
995	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
996	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
997	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
998	  % NOTE (iter-164): the verbatim Milne Prop 3.9 statement+proof quotes are the prior-session-verified
999	  % copies also carried on \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} below. This host lacks a PDF renderer
1000	  % (pdftoppm) so the quotes were NOT re-rendered from references/abelian-varieties.pdf this session;
1001	  % they are reproduced from the verified in-tree copy. See the writer report.
1002	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1003	
1004	  \textbf{Off the genus-$0$ critical path (iter-164).} This $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$
1005	  lemma is \emph{superseded by the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut}
1006	  (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}), which closes the
1007	  base case without it. It is retained only as the classical Milne Proposition~3.9 ($\mathbb
1008	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility) argument feeding the alternative route
1009	  \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}; no block on the genus-$0$ critical path depends on it.
1010	
1011	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$, and let
1012	  $\varphi \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ be a homomorphism of group objects (so $\varphi(0) = 0_A$ and
1013	  $\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)$). Then $\varphi$ is the trivial homomorphism: it
1014	  factors through the terminal object as the constant morphism at the identity,
1015	  $\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1016	
1017	  \medskip
1018	  \noindent\textbf{Equivalent completeness phrasing.} Equivalently: a homomorphism from an
1019	  \emph{affine connected} group object into a \emph{complete} group object is trivial. That is the
1020	  abstract content; the proof below realises it characteristic-free.
1021	\end{lemma}
1022	
1023	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
1024	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
1025	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
1026	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
1027	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
1028	\begin{proof}
1029	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
1030	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1031	  Write $A$ additively (an abelian variety is commutative). We give the conceptual
1032	  \emph{completeness} proof as the abstract argument, then record Milne's elementary $\mathbb
1033	  G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility, which applies whenever $\varphi$ extends to a morphism on
1034	  $\mathbb P^1$ (as it does in the genus-$0$ application).
1035	
1036	  \textbf{Completeness proof.} The image $\varphi(\mathbb G_a)$ is a connected subgroup of $A$
1037	  (image of the connected $\mathbb G_a$ under a homomorphism). As the image of a homomorphism of
1038	  algebraic groups it is a \emph{closed} subgroup of $A$, hence \emph{complete} (closed in the
1039	  complete $A$); as a homomorphic image of the \emph{affine} $\mathbb G_a$ it is moreover
1040	  \emph{affine}. A connected variety that is simultaneously complete and affine over $\bar k$ is a
1041	  single point: its ring of global regular functions is a field finite over $\bar k$, hence equal
1042	  to $\bar k$ (the $\Gamma = \bar k$ collapse already packaged for proper integral sources in
1043	  \cref{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}). Therefore $\varphi(\mathbb G_a)$ is one point;
1044	  being a homomorphism, $\varphi$ sends $0$ there, so the point is $0_A$ and
1045	  $\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A$.
1046	
1047	  \medskip
1048	  \noindent\textbf{Milne's elementary $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility (when $\varphi$
1049	  extends to $\mathbb P^1$).} Suppose $\varphi = f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ for a morphism
1050	  $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ (the genus-$0$ situation). Set $g := f - f(1)$, so $g(1) = 0_A$, and
1051	  form $h_\times := \sigma_\times \fatsemi g \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A$ with the
1052	  scaling companion $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}. Applying the additive
1053	  decomposition \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (complete),
1054	  $W = \mathbb G_m$, base points $1 \in \mathbb P^1$ and $1 \in \mathbb G_m$ (so
1055	  $h_\times(1, 1) = g(1) = 0_A$), the axis restrictions are $x \mapsto g(x)$ and
1056	  $\lambda \mapsto g(\lambda)$, whence $g(\lambda x) = g(x) + g(\lambda)$ on $\bar k^\times$;
1057	  re-expanding $g$ gives the multiplicative relation
1058	  $\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c$ with $c = -\varphi(1)$. Now $\varphi$ is at once
1059	  additive, $\varphi(x + y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y)$, and ``multiplicative-additive'',
1060	  $\varphi(xy) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(y) + c$. Computing $\varphi((x + x')\,y)$ in two ways for
1061	  $x, x' \in \bar k$, $y \in \bar k^\times$,
1062	  \[ \varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(xy) + \varphi(x'y) = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') + 2\varphi(y) + 2c, \]
1063	  while also $\varphi((x+x')y) = \varphi(x+x') + \varphi(y) + c = \varphi(x) + \varphi(x') +
1064	  \varphi(y) + c$; equating gives $\varphi(y) = -c = \varphi(1)$ for every $y \in \bar k^\times$.
1065	  Hence $\varphi$ is constant on the dense $\mathbb G_m \subset \mathbb G_a$, so constant on
1066	  $\mathbb G_a$ (density, $A$ separated), with value $\varphi(0) = 0_A$. This is Milne's argument
1067	  verbatim and uses only the proven \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} plus the scaling action,
1068	  avoiding the ``image is a closed subgroup'' input of the completeness proof. \qedhere
1069	\end{proof}
1070	
1071	\begin{remark}
1072	  \label{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}
1073	  \textbf{The primary route is the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut; why no Theorem~3.2 and no
1074	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ are needed.} The genus-$0$ base case ``$f \colon \mathbb P^1
1075	  \to A$ is constant'' is closed \emph{directly} by the proven additive decomposition
1076	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} composed with a total $\mathbb G$-action on $\mathbb P^1$,
1077	  with \emph{no} appeal to Milne's Theorem~3.2 / Lemma~3.3 (rational-map extension), the theorem of
1078	  the cube, Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials. The reason the earlier draft thought
1079	  Theorem~3.2 was needed --- that the additive defect $\psi(x,y) = f(x+y) - f(x) - f(y)$ lives only
1080	  on the affine $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$ and must be extended over $\mathbb P^1 \times
1081	  \mathbb P^1$ --- evaporates once one composes with a \emph{total} action morphism of
1082	  \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}: the composite is already a total morphism out of $\mathbb P^1 \times
1083	  \mathbb G$, and the complete factor is $\mathbb P^1$, exactly what
1084	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (whose Lean signature needs only the first factor
1085	  complete) consumes.
1086	
1087	  \medskip
1088	  \noindent\textbf{Primary route --- the scaling shortcut (strategy decision of iter-164).} The
1089	  \emph{multiplicative} scaling action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
1090	  \mathbb P^1$, $(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}), closes the base case
1091	  in one shot, \emph{without} the additive homomorphism step or \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial} at
1092	  all. Normalise $f(0) = 0_A$ (translate; here $0$ is a scaling fixed point). Form $h_\times :=
1093	  \sigma_\times \fatsemi f$ and apply \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with $V = \mathbb P^1$,
1094	  $W = \mathbb G_m$, base points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$ and $1 \in \mathbb G_m$ (so $h_\times(0, 1) =
1095	  f(0) = 0_A$). Because $0$ is a \emph{fixed point} of scaling, the $W$-axis restriction collapses:
1096	  $\lambda \mapsto h_\times(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$. The decomposition
1097	  therefore reads $h_\times = p \fatsemi f$ with the second summand the group identity, i.e.
1098	  \[ \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A,
1099	     \qquad\text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''}. \]

1100	  Precomposing with $\lambda \mapsto (1, \lambda)$ gives $f|_{\mathbb G_m} = \text{const}\,f(1)$;
1101	  since $\mathbb G_m$ is dense in $\mathbb P^1$ and $A$ is separated, $f$ is constant on $\mathbb
1102	  P^1$ (the dominant-source/separated-target handle \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}, i.e.\ the
1103	  $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$ family already used by \texttt{rigidity\_core}). This uses
1104	  \emph{only} the proven \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, the total morphism $\sigma_\times$,
1105	  and density --- no $\mathbb G_a$ additivity, no \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, and in
1106	  particular \emph{no} ``image of a group homomorphism is a closed subgroup'' input (a deep Mathlib
1107	  gap). It is the least-Mathlib-blocked realisation of the base case and is the route the live
1108	  consumer \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} formalises.
1109	
1110	  \medskip
1111	  \noindent\textbf{Retained alternative --- the $\mathbb G_a$-additive route.} The Milne-faithful
1112	  argument routes through the translation action $\sigma \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to
1113	  \mathbb P^1$: $\sigma \fatsemi f$ is total, the additive decomposition makes $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ a
1114	  homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$ (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}), and a homomorphism from the
1115	  additive group into an abelian variety is trivial (\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, Milne's
1116	  $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility, Proposition~3.9). This reaches the same conclusion but
1117	  needs the triviality lemma; it is retained for fidelity to Milne and is \emph{off} the genus-$0$
1118	  critical path.
1119	\end{remark}
1120	
1121	\begin{lemma}
1122	[A pointed $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant (Milne Prop 3.9, via the 4th route)]
1123	  \label{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}
1124	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_Ga_to_av_const}
1125	  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1126	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
1127	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
1128	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1129	
1130	  \textbf{Off the genus-$0$ critical path (iter-164): retained alternative route.} This
1131	  $\mathbb G_a$-additive close of the base case is \emph{superseded by the $\mathbb G_m$-scaling
1132	  shortcut} now installed as the proof of \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} (see
1133	  \cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). It is retained as the classical Milne Proposition~3.9
1134	  argument (faithful to the $\mathbb G_a \to A$ homomorphism / $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$
1135	  presentation), but the live consumer \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} no longer depends on
1136	  it; it is provided here only for completeness.
1137	
1138	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over $\bar k$ and $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ a
1139	  morphism with $f(0) = 0_A$ (the additive-identity $\bar k$-point of $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1
1140	  \subset \mathbb P^1$; any base point reduces to this by a normalising translation in $A$).
1141	  Then $f$ is constant. Concretely: the translation action $\sigma$ (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1})
1142	  makes $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ an additive-group homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$, and a homomorphism
1143	  $\mathbb G_a \to A$ is trivial (\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}), so $f$ collapses to the
1144	  constant $0_A$.
1145	\end{lemma}
1146	
1147	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
1148	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
1149	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
1150	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
1151	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
1152	\begin{proof}
1153	  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1154	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
1155	  Normalise $f(0) = 0_A$ by a translation in $A$ (un-translate at the end). The proof is the
1156	  \emph{4th route} (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}): the additive defect map is never built on
1157	  the affine surface and never extended; instead we precompose $f$ with the \emph{total}
1158	  translation action $\sigma$, and the complete factor is $\mathbb P^1$ itself, so the proven
1159	  additive decomposition applies directly. Milne's Theorem~3.2
1160	  (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is \emph{not} used.
1161	
1162	  \textbf{$f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ is an additive homomorphism, via the total action $\sigma$.} Form
1163	  \[ h := \sigma \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_a \to A, \qquad
1164	     \text{``}h(x, y) = f(x + y)\text{''}, \]
1165	  using the translation action $\sigma$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1}; this is a \emph{total}
1166	  morphism (no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
1167	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
1168	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (proper), $W = \mathbb G_a$, and base
1169	  points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $0 \in \mathbb G_a$, noting $h(0, 0) = f(0) = 0_A$. The axis
1170	  restrictions are $x \mapsto h(x, 0) = f(x)$ and $y \mapsto h(0, y) = f(y)$, so the decomposition
1171	  $h = (p \fatsemi f) + (q \fatsemi (f|_{\mathbb G_a}))$ reads, on $\bar k$-points,
1172	  \[ f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), \qquad x \in \mathbb P^1(\bar k),\ y \in \mathbb G_a(\bar k) = \bar k. \]
1173	  Restricting $x$ to $\mathbb G_a$ shows $f|_{\mathbb G_a} \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ is a
1174	  homomorphism of group objects (it sends $0 \mapsto 0_A$ and respects addition).
1175	
1176	  \textbf{A homomorphism $\mathbb G_a \to A$ is trivial.} By \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}
1177	  (the $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ lemma, the content of Milne's $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$
1178	  incompatibility), $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ is the constant morphism at $0_A$. Hence $f$ is constant on
1179	  the dense open $\mathbb G_a \subset \mathbb P^1$; since $\mathbb G_a$ is dense and $A$ is
1180	  separated, $f$ is constant on all of $\mathbb P^1$, with value $f(0) = 0_A$ (i.e.\ the original
1181	  base value before the normalising translation). \qedhere
1182	
1183	  \medskip
1184	  \noindent\emph{Note: this route is the demoted alternative.} The primary base-case proof is the
1185	  $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut now installed directly as the proof of
1186	  \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} (\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}): the multiplicative
1187	  scaling action $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1} gives $\sigma_\times \fatsemi f =
1188	  \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f$ (the $W$-axis collapses because $0$ is a scaling fixed point), so
1189	  $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is constant and $f$ is constant by density --- bypassing both the $\mathbb
1190	  G_a$-additivity step and \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, and avoiding the ``image is a closed
1191	  subgroup'' Mathlib gap. The present $\mathbb G_a$-additive lemma is retained only for fidelity to
1192	  Milne; it is not on the genus-$0$ critical path.
1193	\end{proof}

1195	\section{Morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety are constant}
1196	
1197	\begin{proposition}
1198	
1199	\leanok
1200	[A morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant]
1201	  \label{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}
1202	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const}
1203	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}
1204	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.10 (specialised to a single $\mathbb P^1$ factor), \S I.3, doc p.~20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 26)
1205	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.10. Every rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$
1206	  % from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is constant."
1207	  % SOURCE QUOTE (unirational, same page): "A variety $V$ over an algebraically closed field
1208	  % is said to be unirational if there is a dominating rational map $\mathbb A^n
1209	  % \dashrightarrow V$ with $n = \dim V$; equivalently, if $k(V)$ can be embedded into
1210	  % $k(X_1, \ldots, X_n)$ (pure transcendental extension of $k$). A variety $V$ over an
1211	  % arbitrary field $k$ is said to be unirational if $V_{k^{al}}$ is unirational."
1212	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.10 (case $V = \mathbb P^1$).}
1213	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $A$ an abelian variety over $\bar k$. Then every
1214	  morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant: $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$ is a
1215	  single $\bar k$-point.
1216	\end{proposition}
1217	
1218	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. We may suppose that $k$ is algebraically closed. By assumption
1219	% there is a rational map $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ with dense image, and the composite
1220	% of this with $\alpha$ extends to a morphism $\beta \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \cdots \times
1221	% \mathbb P^1 \to A$. An induction argument, starting from Corollary 1.5, shows that there are
1222	% regular maps $\beta_i \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ such that $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum
1223	% \beta_i(x_i)$, and the lemma shows that each $\beta_i$ is constant."
1224	\begin{proof}
1225	  \uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product, def:gaTranslationP1, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}
1226	  \leanok
1227	  This is the single-factor ($d = 1$) instance of Milne's Proposition~3.10, where the
1228	  unirational variety is $\mathbb P^1$ itself. We give the project's \textbf{primary} proof, the
1229	  \emph{$\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut}: it proves constancy \emph{in one shot} from the proven
1230	  additive decomposition (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and a scaling fixed point,
1231	  without the intermediate ``$f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ additive'' step and \emph{without} the
1232	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$ triviality lemma. The theorem of the cube is \emph{not} used
1233	  (\cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}), and the map $f$ is already a morphism out of the complete
1234	  nonsingular $\mathbb P^1$, so Milne's Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is
1235	  \emph{not} invoked either.
1236	
1237	  \textbf{Reduction to a base point.} Choose the scaling fixed point $0 \in \mathbb G_a \subset
1238	  \mathbb P^1$ (one of the two fixed points of $\sigma_\times$). Translating in the group $A$ by
1239	  $-f(0)$, we may assume $f(0) = 0_A$; un-translating at the end restores the original value.
1240	
1241	  \textbf{The scaling shortcut.} Form the \emph{total} morphism
1242	  \[ h := \sigma_\times \fatsemi f \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A, \qquad
1243	     \text{``}h(x, \lambda) = f(\lambda x)\text{''}, \]
1244	  using the multiplicative scaling action $\sigma_\times$ of \cref{def:gaTranslationP1} (a genuine
1245	  total morphism --- no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
1246	  \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
1247	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with $V = \mathbb P^1$ (proper), $W = \mathbb G_m$, and base
1248	  points $0 \in \mathbb P^1$, $1 \in \mathbb G_m$; here $h(0, 1) = f(1 \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$, as
1249	  required. The decomposition writes $h = (p \fatsemi f_0) + (q \fatsemi g)$ with $f_0$ the
1250	  $V$-axis restriction $x \mapsto h(x, 1) = f(1 \cdot x) = f(x)$ (so $f_0 = f$) and $g$ the
1251	  $W$-axis restriction $\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A$.
1252	
1253	  \textbf{The $W$-axis collapses (the load-bearing scaling fixed point).} Because $0$ is a
1254	  \emph{fixed point} of scaling, $\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0$ for all $\lambda$, so the $W$-axis
1255	  restriction $g$ is the \emph{constant} morphism at $0_A$ --- the identity of the hom-group
1256	  $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_m, A)$. The corresponding summand $q \fatsemi g$ is therefore the group
1257	  identity, and the decomposition collapses to
1258	  \[ h = p \fatsemi f, \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
1259	     \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f, \qquad
1260	     \text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''} \quad (x \in \mathbb P^1,\ \lambda \in \mathbb G_m). \]
1261	
1262	  \textbf{Constancy via density.} Specialise $x = 1$ (precompose with $\lambda \mapsto (1,
1263	  \lambda)$): $f(\lambda) = f(1)$ for all $\lambda \in \mathbb G_m$, i.e.\ $f|_{\mathbb G_m}$ is
1264	  the constant morphism at $f(1)$. Since $\mathbb G_m$ is dense in the irreducible $\mathbb P^1$
1265	  and $A$ is separated, the two morphisms $f$ and $\mathrm{const}_{f(1)}$, agreeing on the
1266	  non-empty open $\mathbb G_m$, agree everywhere by the dominant-source/separated-target rigidity
1267	  handle \cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (the same \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant} family
1268	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} uses). Hence $f$ is constant, with value $f(1)$; in the normalised
1269	  picture $f(0) = 0_A$, so the constant is $0_A$ (the original $f(0)$ before translation).
1270	
1271	  \medskip
1272	  \noindent\emph{Multi-factor remark.} For the general unirational statement of Proposition~3.10,
1273	  one writes $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)$ via the additive decomposition
1274	  (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and applies this base case to each $\beta_i \colon
1275	  \mathbb P^1 \to A$; we need only the $d = 1$ case here. The Milne-faithful $\mathbb
1276	  G_a$-additive route (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}, via $\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0$,
1277	  \cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}) reaches the same conclusion and is retained as an alternative,
1278	  but is \emph{not} on this critical path.
1279	\end{proof}

14	`ProjectiveLineBar`, `Gm` (with `Ga` as the demoted-route companion), and
15	`gmScalingP1 : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`, in particular: which Mathlib API does each
16	extend (Proj? AffineSpace? a from-scratch Spec? a typeclass? a bare morphism?),

41	### Decision D1: realization of `ProjectiveLineBar`
42	
43	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj 𝒜` of a graded ring, specialised to `𝒜 = ℕ → Submodule k̄ (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` (the standard ℕ-grading by total degree). The two charts `𝔸¹ = D₊(X₀)` and `ℙ¹ \ {0} = D₊(X₁)` are then `Proj.awayι` images. Cite: `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Scheme:?` (Proj), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic:?` (Proj.awayι, Proj.affineOpenCover), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper:366` (IsProper).
44	- **Project's current path**: abstract proxy `P1 : Over (Spec kbar)` with the curve-typeclass bundle `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1, IsProper, GeometricallyIrreducible, genus = 0]`. The proxy is genuinely abstract — there is no concrete underlying scheme.
45	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The abstract proxy *cannot* define a chartwise morphism (`σ_×`) — it has no charts. It also cannot provide an `𝔸¹` chart on which 𝔾ₘ is the dense open subset. So the proxy must be replaced by a concrete construction once we want `gmScalingP1` to exist as a Lean term.
46	- **Cost of divergence**: the proxy approach forces `gmScalingP1` to remain a `sorry`-stubbed black box; no chartwise definition, no `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` proof. The whole 𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut becomes unprovable. The Proj realization, by contrast, gives `IsProper` free and gives the two affine charts as actual `Spec` schemes the prover can compute on.
47	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — define `ProjectiveLineBar : Over (Spec (.of kbar))` as the base change to `Spec k̄` of `Proj 𝒜` for `𝒜` the standard ℕ-grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` (or pulled back from `Spec ℤ` if upstreaming is in scope). The two charts are then `Proj.awayι 𝒜 X₀ …` and `Proj.awayι 𝒜 X₁ …`, both of which are `Spec` of an explicit `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` — exactly what the chartwise σ_× definition needs.
48	  - `[IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom]` — FREE (`IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜)` for `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ k̄[X₀,X₁]`).
49	  - `[GeometricallyIrreducible ProjectiveLineBar.hom]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 _]`: project-side sub-proofs. Both are sub-builds (no Mathlib instance for `Proj`). Marked `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL` *inside* this `ALIGN` verdict.
50	  - `genus ProjectiveLineBar = 0` — still blocked on Riemann–Roch (same as the proxy path), but at least the statement is concrete.

52	### Decision D2: realization of `Gm` and `Ga` as group objects
53	
54	- **Mathlib idiom**: install `GrpObj` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` from `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_:35`, presenting the Yoneda-functor of groups (units / additive group of global sections). Use Mathlib's `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec k̄)` for the underlying scheme of `Ga`, and `Spec (.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit k̄)))` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) for `Gm`. Use `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth:38`) to derive `Smooth` for free.
55	- **Project's current path**: not yet built — the blueprint says `AffineSpace 𝔸(1; Spec k̄)` for `Ga` and "𝔾_m = 𝔸¹ \ {0}" for `Gm`, both with a `GrpObj` instance. No body yet.
56	- **Gap**: in-proposal — there is no shipped code to compare against. The blueprint's *idea* (use `AffineSpace` for `Ga`, the basic-open of `𝔸¹` for `Gm`) is the right *direction* but has two sub-decisions where Mathlib has a clear preferred shape:
57	  1. **Direct vs. Yoneda installation of `GrpObj`.** Mathlib's idiom (used internally for the projection of representability) is `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`. The blueprint isn't explicit about which approach. ⇒ pick `ofRepresentableBy`.
58	  2. **`Gm` as `(AffineSpace _).basicOpen (coord _ 0)` vs. `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`.** Both are isomorphic schemes; only the second is *directly affine*. The second is far cleaner for installing `GrpObj` (it has `IsAffine` for free; basic-opens carry over the structure-sheaf API via `IsLocalization.Away` but are not affine by definition in Mathlib's API).
59	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** (Mathlib doesn't ship these), with two **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** sub-rules:
60	  - **D2.a — `Ga`**: define `Ga : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))` (or the canonical Over via `AffineSpace.over`). Install `GrpObj Ga` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the additive-group functor `(T ↦ AddGrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤))`, using `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace:155`) as the representable-by witness (S-morphisms `T ⟶ 𝔸(1; S)` ≃ `Γ(T, ⊤)`). DO NOT define the multiplication by `Pi.lift` of an ad-hoc `homOfVector` — that builds a parallel API. The Yoneda installer is cheaper and composes with the rest of Mathlib's `Hom.group` API.
61	  - **D2.b — `Gm`**: define `Gm : Over (Spec (.of kbar))` as the canonical Over of `Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))` — i.e. `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`. Install `GrpObj Gm` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the multiplicative-group functor `(T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ)`, the representable-by witness being the standard "morphism into Spec of away-localization ↔ unit in global sections" bijection. (Mathlib has `IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)` at `AffineScheme:632/651/666` and `Spec_basicOpen` at `AffineScheme:596` — these supply the bijection.) DO NOT define `Gm` as `(AffineSpace _).basicOpen _` — the basic-open path has weaker `IsAffine`/structure-sheaf instances and forces bridge lemmas at every consumer site.
62	  - **D2.c — `Smooth`**: both `Gm.hom` and `Ga.hom` should derive `Smooth` from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed`. Project responsibility is to provide `[LocallyOfFinitePresentation _.hom]` (free for `Ga` from `AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite`; for `Gm` from `IsAffineHom` + `Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ k̄[t,t⁻¹]`) and `[IsReduced _.left]` (free — both `MvPolynomial Unit k̄` and `Localization.Away t` are domains over a field).
63	  - **D2.d — `GeometricallyIrreducible`**: again project-side. Both are `Spec` of an integral domain over `k̄` and base-change to `Spec k̄` is itself (`k̄ = k̄`), so `GeometricallyIntegral` reduces to `IsIntegral` on global sections; conclude via `GeometricallyIntegral → GeometricallyIrreducible` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Integral:58`).
64	
65	### Decision D3: shape of `gmScalingP1`
66	
67	- **Mathlib idiom**: bare morphism `gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar` in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`, defined chartwise by `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing:?`). No `IsAction` / `MulAction` typeclass exists at scheme level in Mathlib — every scheme-level "action" the library knows is a bare morphism plus named property lemmas. Cite as precedent: `GrpObj.mulRight` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_:?`) — a one-sided multiplication packaged as a morphism, no typeclass wrapper.
68	- **Project's current path**: not yet built — the blueprint description is chartwise (`(x, λ) ↦ λx` on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`; `1/(λx) = u/λ` near `∞`), with `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` highlighted as the load-bearing property.
69	- **Gap**: in-proposal. The risk is over-engineering — wrapping σ_× in a typeclass / `MulAction`-style structure that doesn't exist anywhere else in Mathlib.
70	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — define `gmScalingP1` as a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism. Expose the load-bearing property as a separate lemma (named e.g. `gmScalingP1_zero_fixed` or `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`):
71	  ```lean
72	  -- The fixed-point lemma that the rigidity consumer needs:
73	  -- σ_×(0, ·) = const 0, packaged as the W-axis collapse hypothesis
74	  -- of `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`:
75	  lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero :
76	      lift (𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar)
77	        (toUnit ProjectiveLineBar ≫ (zeroPoint : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm)) ≫
78	        ... = ... := by ...
79	  ```
80	  Use `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` over the two `Proj.awayι`-charts × `Gm` (which together cover `ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m` because `Proj.affineOpenCover` does). Each chart-restriction is then a `Spec.map` of an explicit ring map between `k̄[t, λ, λ⁻¹]`-algebras (on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m`: `t ↦ λ·t`; near `∞`: `u ↦ u/λ`). DO NOT introduce a custom typeclass `class IsSchemeMulAction (G H : ...) ...` — there is no Mathlib precedent and the only consumer needs only the bare morphism + the one fixed-point property.
81	  - The `Gluing.glueMorphisms` precondition (agreement on pairwise pullbacks) reduces to an equality of `Spec.map`s on the localization `k̄[t, t⁻¹, λ, λ⁻¹]` — a direct ring-level computation.
82	  - The "𝔾_m as both source and target factor" — only on the source. On the target (ℙ¹) we just hit the chart we like.
83	
84	### Decision D4: file location
85	
86	- **Mathlib idiom**: a small, focused scheme construction with one main downstream consumer gets a short file directly under that consumer. Closest precedent: `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth` (62 lines), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Abelian` (147 lines) — each defines a new instance/lemma in a focused file imported by exactly one or two downstream proofs.
87	- **Project's current path**: AVR.lean is already 992 lines and the new infrastructure (definitions, 4–5 instances, glue lemmas, the chartwise σ_× construction) is ~150–400 LOC.
88	- **Gap**: would not break correctness either way, but inlining 250+ LOC of new scheme infrastructure into the rigidity-proof file makes both the proofs and the infrastructure harder to read and harder to upstream later.
89	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — split into a new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean`, imported by AVR.lean. Keep `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` / `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` / `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in AVR.lean; move `ProjectiveLineBar`, `Ga`, `Gm`, `gmScalingP1`, and their typeclass instances + chart-level helper lemmas to the new file. Rationale: matches Mathlib's "one focused file per scheme construction with ~one downstream consumer" pattern, keeps AVR.lean's rigidity-proof content centred, and leaves a clean unit for upstream PR'ing later (e.g. as `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpace.One` / `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Gm` / `…Group.Ga`).
90	
91	## Recommendation
92	
93	Land the scaffold this iter as:
94	
95	```text
96	AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean       -- NEW FILE
97	  - import AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity-prerequisites
98	    (Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper,
99	     Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace,
100	     Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth,
101	     Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Grp_)
102	  - namespace AlgebraicGeometry
103	  - def standardGrading : ℕ → Submodule k̄ (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)
104	    + GradedRing instance (project-side, but a routine MvPolynomial homogeneous-decomposition)
105	  - def ProjectiveLineBar : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := … (Proj-based)
106	    instance [IsAlgClosed kbar] : IsProper ProjectiveLineBar.hom := … -- FREE from ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper
107	    instance : GeometricallyIrreducible ProjectiveLineBar.hom := sorry
108	    instance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 ProjectiveLineBar.hom := sorry
109	    def zeroPt, onePt, inftyPt : 𝟙_ ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar := … (Proj.awayι-image of the standard k̄-points)
110	  - def Ga : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) |>.asOver _
111	    instance : GrpObj Ga := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy Ga (… additive functor …) (… via AffineSpace.homOverEquiv …)
112	    instance : Smooth Ga.hom := smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed _ (instances inferred)
113	  - def Gm : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (X : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))).asOver _
114	    instance : GrpObj Gm := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy Gm (… units functor …) (… via Spec.map-of-away ↔ unit …)
115	    instance : Smooth Gm.hom := smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed _ (instances inferred)
116	  - def gmScalingP1 : ProjectiveLineBar ⊗ Gm ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar := … (Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms over the two Proj.awayι charts × Gm)
117	  - lemma gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero : lift (toUnit (… ≫ zeroPt)) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1 = toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt := …
118	  - (optional, if/when needed) gmScalingP1_action_id : lift (𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar) (toUnit _ ≫ onePt_of_Gm) ≫ gmScalingP1 = 𝟙 ProjectiveLineBar
119	end AlgebraicGeometry
120	
121	AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean
122	  import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects   -- NEW
123	  -- morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const, genusZero_curve_iso_P1, rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
124	  -- are refactored to take the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar` (no more abstract `(P1 : Over …)` proxy)
125	```
126	
127	The four big shape rules — **(1)** Proj-based ℙ¹ (not abstract proxy, not glued-from-charts directly), **(2)** Yoneda-installed GrpObj via `ofRepresentableBy` (not direct `μ`-by-hand), **(3)** Spec(`k̄[t,t⁻¹]`) for Gm (not basic-open of `𝔸¹`), **(4)** bare morphism for σ_× (not typeclass) — together remove every parallel-API risk the iter-164 progress critic flagged. The infrastructure built this way also stays directly upstream-able to Mathlib later (no `archon-`-specific wrappers to peel off).
128	
129	The work the project still owes regardless of this choice — and which `NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL` for `Proj 𝒜`-side: `GeometricallyIrreducible` and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` of `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜.hom` for `𝒜` = standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`, and `genus ProjectiveLineBar = 0` (Riemann–Roch debt; same on every realization).

8	/-!
9	# Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-`0` curve into an abelian variety are constant
10	
11	This file scaffolds the project's **committed characteristic-free** genus-`0` route
12	(route (c)): the abelian-variety rigidity stack. It sits **upstream** of
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian` (it imports only `AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`), breaking the
14	`RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian` import cycle so that `genusZeroWitness` can consume the
15	genus-`0` rigidity keystone directly.
16	
17	The headline `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` is the char-free replacement (no
18	`[CharZero kbar]`) for `AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`
19	(which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
20	
21	The minimal chain has four links:
22	
23	1. `rigidity_lemma` — the Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I): the cube-free, cohomology-free
24	   properness/closed-map entry point. **PROVEN axiom-clean** (iters 157–162). The whole chain is
25	   `sorry`-free: bridge 1 is `snd_left_isClosedMap`; bridge 2
26	   (`rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, the slice-constancy / affine-constancy equation) is
27	   assembled from Step 2 `morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints` and Step 1
28	   `rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, whose deep *algebraic* content is
29	   `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral` and whose *geometric* slice/section assembly (the
30	   `IsIntegral X.left` retract argument, `isIntegral_of_retract`) is also closed.
31	2. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
32	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
33	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
34	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
35	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
36	3. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
37	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
38	4. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
39	
40	See `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` for the informal sketches and sources
41	(Mumford, *Abelian Varieties*, Ch. II §4, §6; Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10;
42	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
43	
44	## Encoding notes (iter-157 scaffold)
45	
46	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so — following the established project idiom
47	(see `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`) — the projective line is encoded by its abstract
48	characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of relative
49	dimension `1` with `genus = 0`. The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional**
50	(`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled.
51	Declaration 4 is pinned verbatim to `rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`,
52	because it is the exact signature the consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/
